western.definition: Codified, transmissible information; verified through replication; abstracted from context.

relational.definition: Embodied understanding of relationships; verified through practical effectiveness; context-dependent and experiential.

functional_comparison.relational: Bodies, practices, land, writings, databases of diverse types
Apprenticeship, doing, teaching and reading environments and books
Water-as-teacher, energy-as-teacher, biology, astronomy, multigenerational observations

hidden_variables.name: Relational context
hidden_variables.description: The web of relationships in which the knowledge was acquired and is applied.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Abstraction strips context as a feature, not a loss.

hidden_variables.name: Embodied skill
hidden_variables.description: Knowledge that lives in the practiced body, not in symbols.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Codification converts what can be written, leaving the rest invisible.

hidden_variables.name: Timing sensitivity
hidden_variables.description: Knowledge of when, not just what — seasonal, developmental, situational.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Decontextualized facts erase their proper moment.

hidden_variables.name: Field awareness
hidden_variables.description: Knowledge of the surrounding conditions that the explicit content takes for granted.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Abstraction assumes the field is constant.

translation_protocol.when_to_translate: When 'knowledge' is being equated with what can be written down, and embodied or context-dependent knowing is being treated as folklore.
translation_protocol.how: Ask: 'What conditions does this knowledge assume? What practices keep it alive? What can be learned without those?'
